channel.title: The Cambridge Record — budget
channel.description: Everything on the record about budget.

item.title: CC 2026-54 — The Finance Committee held a public hearing on Tuesday, May 5, 2026 on the City and School budgets covering the fiscal p
item.description: The Finance Committee held a public hearing on Tuesday, May 5, 2026 on the City and School budgets covering the fiscal period July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2027.

item.title: CC 2026-56 — The Finance Committee held a public hearing on Tuesday, May 12, 2026 on the City and School budgets covering the fiscal
item.description: The Finance Committee held a public hearing on Tuesday, May 12, 2026 on the City and School budgets covering the fiscal period July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2027.

item.title: COF 2026-82 — Communication from Finance co-chairs: Process for Identifying Future Budget Priorities, Part 3
item.description: Communication from Finance co-chairs: Process for Identifying Future Budget Priorities, Part 3

item.title: CMA 2026-166 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to amendments to the FY27 Submitted General Fund Bu
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to amendments to the FY27 Submitted General Fund Budget.

item.title: CMA 2026-154 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a cross statutory transfer of $322,825.00 within
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a cross statutory transfer of $322,825.00 within the School Department General Fund.

item.title: POR 2026-99 — That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to work with the Mayor’s Office, Law Department, Election Commission, a
item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to work with the Mayor’s Office, Law Department, Election Commission, and other relevant City Departments to engage the Collins Center in assisting the City in reviewing policy options for allowing Cambridge voters to directly elect the City’s Mayor.

item.title: COF 2026-74 — A communication from Mayor Siddiqui transmitting a memorandum regarding information from the School Committee
item.description: A communication from Mayor Siddiqui transmitting a memorandum regarding information from the School Committee

item.title: CMA 2026-93 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to submission of the FY2027 budget and appropriatio
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to submission of the FY2027 budget and appropriation orders for the General Fund, Water Fund, and Public Investment Fund.

item.title: CMA 2026-91 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 26-20 regarding a re
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 26-20 regarding a report on major capital initiatives at or above $15,000,000 for the period from FY21 to FY26.

item.description: Minutes of the Joint Roundtable/Working Meeting of the Cambridge City Council and the School Committee was held on Wednesday, March 4, 2026.

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to recommendations for the block rates for water consumption and sewer use for the period beginning April 1, 2026 and ending March 31, 2027.

item.title: CC 2026-27 — The Finance Committee held a public meeting on February 3, 2026 to review and discuss the Operating Budget in advance of
item.description: The Finance Committee held a public meeting on February 3, 2026 to review and discuss the Operating Budget in advance of the FY27 budget season.

item.description: The Finance Committee held a public meeting on February 11, 2026 to review and discuss the Capital Budget and Public Investment Planning in advance of the FY27 budget season.

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the City of Cambridge retaining its Triple A (AAA) rating from the nation's three major credit rating agencies.

item.title: COF 2026-15 — A COMMUNICATION HAS BEEN RECEIVED FROM DAVID MURPHY, SUPERINTENDENT CAMBRIDGE PUBLIC SCHOOLS. REGARDING PRIORITIES DRIVI
item.description: A COMMUNICATION HAS BEEN RECEIVED FROM DAVID MURPHY, SUPERINTENDENT CAMBRIDGE PUBLIC SCHOOLS. REGARDING PRIORITIES DRIVING OUR FY27 OPERATING BUDGET FOR THE CAMBRIDGE PUBLIC SCHOOLS

item.title: POR 2026-34 — That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which C
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which Cambridge can expand free early child care offerings, including models for means-tested programming, and exploration of non-City funding sources, from the state or foundations. POR

item.description: An Ordinance has been received from Interim City Clerk, relative to Fresh Pond Golf Course Revolving Fund Amendments to Section 3.24.070 – Authorized Revolving Funds. PASSED TO A SECOND READING IN COUNCIL JUNE 9, 2025 ELIGIBLE TO BE ORDAINED JUNE 30, 2025

item.title: CMA 2025 #151 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to an amendment to the Fresh Pond Golf Pro Shop Fun
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to an amendment to the Fresh Pond Golf Pro Shop Fund ordinance to enable the funds to be used to cover the purchase of office supplies for the shop and the purchase of supplies and services for the care, maintenance, and improvemen

item.title: CMA 2025 #134 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to following amendments to the FY26 Submitted Gener
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to following amendments to the FY26 Submitted General Fund Budget. These amendments to the Budget reflect changes requested by the City Council based on feedback and discussions during public hearings on the FY26 Operating and Capi

item.title: CMA 2025 #133 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a proposed increase in the FY26 budget of $1 mil
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a proposed increase in the FY26 budget of $1 million that would create new municipal vouchers and supportive services for people who are unhoused and a $5 million free cash appropriation for a Federal Grant Stabilization Fund

item.title: AR-25-31 — That the City Manager is requested to include in the FY26 Operating Budget a continued commitment to Emergency Housing V
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to include in the FY26 Operating Budget a continued commitment to Emergency Housing Vouchers for Permanent Supportive Housing and Mixed Status Families, and the Transition Wellness Center, as well as allocate the necessary resources to establish a municipal success

item.description: Nike Tirman, regarding STOP THESE AGEIST POLICIES PLEASE ACT PROMPTLY to preserve parking on Broadway!

item.title: POR 2025 #69 — That the City Manager is requested to include in the FY26 Operating Budget a continued commitment to Emergency Housing V
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to include in the FY26 Operating Budget a continued commitment to Emergency Housing Vouchers for Permanent Supportive Housing and Mixed Status Families, and the Transition Wellness Center, as well as allocate the necessary resources to establish a municipal success

item.description: Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the FY 2026 submitted budget and appropriation orders for the General Fund, Water Fund, and Public Investment Fund. PLACED ON UNFINISHED BUSINESS AND REFERRED TO THE FINANCE COMMITTEE IN COUNCIL APRIL 28, 2025

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the City of Cambridge retaining its AAA rating from the nation's three major credit rating agencies

item.title: POR 2025 #21 — That the City Manager is requested to work with all appropriate Departments to develop language to create an “Affordable
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to work with all appropriate Departments to develop language to create an “Affordable Rent Incentive Program” program for Cambridge and to report back to the City Council with option, including the percentage of possible tax abatements

item.title: CMA 2025 #31 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 24-63 regarding rec
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 24-63 regarding recognizing and honoring the Massachusett Tribe

item.title: POR 2025 #9 — That the City Council meeting scheduled for February 24, 2025 shall be designated as a Joint Roundtable with the School
item.description: That the City Council meeting scheduled for February 24, 2025 shall be designated as a Joint Roundtable with the School Committee to discuss FY26 Cambridge Public Schools budget priorities

item.title: — CMA 2025 #19 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 24-31
item.description: CMA 2025 #19 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 24-31 regarding an update to the Municipal Facilities Improvement Plan (MFIP) including revised cost estimates to help inform the FY26 and ongoing capital budget priorities

item.description: City Council support of H.2963, An Act relative to payments in lieu of taxation by organizations exempt from the property tax, to ensure municipalities are fairly compensated and their long-term budgetary needs are preserved. item.description: That the City Manager is hereby directed to confer with the City Manager’s Housing Liaison, Community Development Department, and the Cambridge Housing Authority on the feasibility of municipally-funded housing vouchers

item.title: POR 2023 #101 — That City Manager is requested to adjust the Health Alliance Budget to ensure that all services provided to the City of
item.description: That City Manager is requested to adjust the Health Alliance Budget to ensure that all services provided to the City of Cambridge by the Health Alliance for Public Health Department needs and staff are fully funded, and to adjust the Capital Budget to ensure all necessary repairs are made to the Win

item.title: POR 2023 #102 — That the City Manager is requested to increase the Affordable Housing Trust budget in FY24 by 7.1% to equal the increase
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to increase the Affordable Housing Trust budget in FY24 by 7.1% to equal the increase of the overall city budget, increase the Affordable Housing Trust budget by $20 million through a free cash appropriation, outside of the FY24 budget, and engage in creating a 3-y

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to amendments to the FY24 Submitted General Fund Budget

item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to increase the Affordable Housing Trust budget in FY24 by 7.1% to equal the increase of the overall city budget

item.title: POR 2023 #112 — That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to increase the Affordable Housing Trust budget by $20 million through
item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to increase the Affordable Housing Trust budget by $20 million through a free cash appropriation, outside of the FY24 budget

item.title: POR 2023 #113 — That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to engage in creating a 3-year plan to increase the Affordable Housing
item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to engage in creating a 3-year plan to increase the Affordable Housing Trust budget to 10% of the overall City budget

item.title: CMA 2023 #105 — Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the FY2024 submitted budget and appropriation ord
item.description: Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the FY2024 submitted budget and appropriation orders.

item.title: POR 2023 #47 — That the City Manager is requested to confer with the City Manager’s Housing Liaison, Community Development Department,
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to confer with the City Manager’s Housing Liaison, Community Development Department, Cambridge Housing Authority, and the City Solicitor on the feasibility of municipally-funded housing vouchers

item.title: POR 2023 #48 — That the City Manager is requested to fund the HEART initiative and negotiate a contract for services with HEART, to inc
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to fund the HEART initiative and negotiate a contract for services with HEART, to include but not limited to HEART responding to certain 911 calls. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR NOLAN IN COUNCIL MARCH 6, 2023
